title: fragaria vesca subvar. minor
intro: Fragaria vesca subvar. minor, also known as the woodland strawberry, is a subspecies of wild strawberry native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The woodland strawberry has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. Herbal preparations of Fragaria vesca subvar. minor include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
